name: roam-research
title: Roam Research
description: Your Roam graphs at your fingertips! Quickly search, capture and navigate your Roam graphs.
author: roamresearch

commands.name: index
commands.title: Roam Research Index Command
commands.description: Index Command which you can use to explore other commands or uninstall graphs from Raycast

commands.name: random
commands.title: Random Note
commands.description: View a random note from your graph/graphs

commands.name: search
commands.title: Search Note
commands.description: Search across all your Roam graphs

commands.name: new-graph
commands.title: Add Graph
commands.description: Install a new Roam graph into this Raycast extension

commands.name: quick-capture
commands.title: Quick Capture
commands.description: Quickly append notes to your Roam graph

commands.name: instant-capture-default-graph
commands.title: Instant Capture
commands.description: Zero-UI capture using your designated Instant Capture template

commands.name: manage-templates
commands.title: Manage Capture Templates
commands.description: Create, edit, and delete capture templates for your Roam graphs

commands.name: create-graph-quicklink
commands.title: Create Graph Quicklink
commands.description: Create a Raycast Quicklink for a Roam graph or a specific page in that graph

commands.name: outbox-view
commands.title: Capture Outbox
commands.description: View capture history and retry failed captures

commands.name: outbox-retry
commands.title: Sync Outbox
commands.description: Retry pending captures in the outbox queue

preferences.name: openIn
preferences.title: Open in...
preferences.description: Choose where to open Roam pages/blocks.

preferences.name: hideCodeBlocksInSearch
preferences.title: Search
preferences.label: Hide code blocks in search?
preferences.description: Check if you want to hide code blocks in the search command

preferences.name: quickCaptureTemplate
preferences.title: (DEPRECATED) Default template for Quick Capture
preferences.label: (DEPRECATED) Default template for Quick Capture
preferences.description:
{date} supports {date: HH:mm:ss} (by default), 
                           {date: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss}, 
                           all formats in dayjs
You can have multiple lines for multiple blocks. For those, use a single space for indentation

preferences.name: quickCaptureTagTodayDnp
preferences.title: Tag Quick Capture with Today's Daily Note
preferences.label: When sending to a non daily notes page
preferences.description: Check if you want to append your quick captures with something like #[[August 1, 2023]]
